overview:
Build a new sibling package `@symphony/worker-box-pool` (ts/packages/worker-box-pool) implementing a long-lived, reload-surviving `BoxPool` that produces the `workerHost: string | null` value the orchestrator already threads end to end. The pool composes a swappable `BoxProvider` (fake / static-ssh / cloud) behind one interface, a single serial reaper loop, owner-stamped idempotent leases (leaseId), and a cloud-only write-ahead ledger. It is wired through the runtime's existing FUNCTION-INJECTION style (like removeIssueWorkspaces/appendLogEvent via SymphonyRuntimeOptions), never the ports DI scheme, and never extends the reverse-MCP-tunnel `@symphony/worker-host-pool`.

This revision REMOVES the warm/teardown-via-hooks abstraction entirely. VERIFIED in code: `createWorkspaceForIssue` (workspace/src/index.ts:25-51) already runs `hooks.afterCreate` internally, keyed by (issue, slotIndex, ensembleSize), and the per-run workspace is created inside `runAgentAttempt` (agent-runner/src/index.ts:98-106). A box-level `afterCreate` at warm time would DOUBLE-FIRE arbitrary operator bash. So the pool's `warm()`/`teardown()` are box-LIFECYCLE only (provision readiness via probe; teardown = provider.destroy), and NEVER run workspace hooks. The runner remains the sole owner of workspace/hook lifecycle. `SyncStrategy` is reduced to an internal `WarmupStrategy` that only gates readiness (probe loop), with no hook execution.

The lease is acquired inside `runClaim` right before `this.runner(...)` and released/failed in the SAME `finally` that already calls `handle.release()` (runtime/src/index.ts:509-511), reusing every generation-cleanup / abort / stall path. CRITICAL FIXES grounded in verified code: (1) affinity is threaded from `RetryEntry.workerHost` (orchestrator:218), NOT from the null bypass placeholder - the bypass writes a `pending://<issueId>/<slot>` sentinel while affinity is read from a separate `claim.affinityHost` carried on RunningEntry. (2) release-vs-fail is decided by a STRUCTURED outcome (a `boxOutcome: 'healthy'|'poison'` flag set by the runtime from typed signals), NOT by substring-matching `ssh_timeout`/`ssh_not_found` - because `ssh_not_found` (ssh/src/index.ts:68,77) is a LOCAL ENOENT (healthy box) and a stall surfaces as `agent_stalled after Nms` (runtime:619) with no ssh_* substring. (3) `stop()` is synchronous void (runtime:301-307) and `main.ts` does not await it (main.ts:125-127); drain is awaited in `main.ts`'s `finally` AFTER `runtime.start()` returns (start() exits when stopped), via a new `runtime.drainBoxPool()` async method, not inside stop(). (4) the reaper timer is explicitly `.unref?.()`-ed by the pool because `systemClock.setTimeout` (ports:41) never unrefs and `TimerHandle.unref` is optional (ports:36). (5) claim-time no_capacity UN-CLAIMS the slot via a new `orchestrator.abandonClaim(issueId, slotIndex)` (inverse of claim, deletes running+claimed with NO retry record) so the next poll re-evaluates via `canAcquire()` - it does NOT call `finish()` (which schedules a backoff retry, orchestrator:207-221). (6) reaper orphan-destroy and runClaim release coordinate through the single leaseId+settled+state guard AND a per-box async mutex so inFlight never underflows. (7) growth single-flight is keyed by a CAPACITY RESERVATION taken synchronously before any await (reserved counter), not by boxId, so two concurrent growth decisions cannot both allocate.

Config lives under `worker.boxPool` (default absent => disabled => byte-identical for all existing configs), parsed with the existing manual-zod + coercion convention, validated at parse time. Default `maxInFlight=1` (the only value safe against the workerHost-keyed MCP tunnel refCount, verified mcp/src/agentEndpoint.ts:106-128 and worker-host-pool/src/index.ts:73-88). Strictly TDD: every task ships failing tests first, gated by `mise run check` with `mise run tidy` for autofix. NOTE: the CRABBOX research input was null - no crabbox artifact exists in the repo; this plan is grounded entirely in the source I read and verified, not transcribed from any crabbox spec.

packageLayout.purpose: New package @symphony/worker-box-pool, version 0.1.0, private, type module, main ./dist/index.js, types ./dist/index.d.ts, exports { '.': './dist/index.js' }. deps: @symphony/domain, @symphony/ports, @symphony/ssh (all workspace:*). NO @symphony/workspace dep (pool does NOT own workspace/hook lifecycle). NO cloud SDK deps. Mirror packages/worker-host-pool/package.json shape.
Project-reference tsconfig mirroring packages/policies/tsconfig.json: composite build, references to @symphony/domain, @symphony/ports, @symphony/ssh.

Public barrel: re-exports createBoxPool, registerBoxProvider, resolveProvider, clearBoxProviderRegistry (test helper), FakeBoxProvider, StaticSshBoxProvider, and all public types. Registers built-in 'fake' + 'static-ssh' providers at module load.

All shared interfaces/types so impl files import them without cycles: BoxProvider, BoxDescriptor, BoxHealth, ProvisionRequest, ProviderCapabilities, ProviderDeps, BoxLease, BoxPool, AcquireRequest, AcquireResult, WarmupStrategy, TeardownReason, BoxState, BoxRecord, LedgerRow, BoxPoolSnapshot, BoxProviderFactory, BoxOutcome ('healthy'|'poison'), Mutex.
Tiny async mutex (promise-chain) used to serialize per-box state mutations AND the reaper-vs-release interleave. runExclusive<T>(fn). No deps.

BoxPool impl: Map<boxId,BoxRecord> inventory, lease table, FIFO waiter queue, a synchronous capacity-reservation counter (reservedProvisions) taken BEFORE any await so concurrent growth cannot exceed max, per-box mutex, spend accounting (concurrentBoxes + box-seconds + dailyBoxSeconds with an explicit reset clock), canAcquire(), acquire(), reconcile(), hydrate(), drain(), snapshot(). Owns the ClockPort reaper timer and calls handle.unref?.() on it.

createLease factory producing BoxLease: per-lease settled flag + leaseId-ownership check on release()/fail(); release(outcome:'healthy') keeps box, fail(reason) marks 'poison' for recycle; heartbeat() updates record.lastHeartbeatMs. Pure functions over a BoxRecord + injected pool callbacks (onSettle taking the per-box mutex). Cross-generation/stale leaseId and DESTROYED state are no-ops that NEVER touch inFlight.

runReaperTick(internals, now): single serial pass guarded by an in-progress flag - force DESTROYING for ttl/idle above min (LEASED only flagged markedForDestroy, recycled when inFlight->0 INSIDE the per-box mutex so it can't race release); probe long-idle, demote unhealthy to DEGRADED; detect orphan leases (lastHeartbeatMs older than staleHeartbeatMs AND no active run, distinguished from a long-but-alive single turn via a separate aliveProbe) and force-return them clearing the lease table + inFlight; reconcile provider.list() (authoritative) vs registry+ledger (advisory) - re-adopt ours, destroy ONLY labeled pool-owned, NEVER unlabeled, mark registered-but-missing DESTROYED; top up toward min/warm under spend budget.

Write-ahead JSON ledger under ledgerPath: flush(rows), load(), upsert(row), delete(boxId). Used ONLY when capabilities.usesLedger (cloud). Atomic write (tmp+rename). Row written BEFORE provision with provisional status, then UPSERTED with providerRef/workerHost after provision returns, so a crash between create and ledger-correlate is recoverable by labels OR provisional boxId. Includes daily-spend persistence: a sidecar spend.json (boxSecondsToday, dayKey) so the daily cap survives restart.

registerBoxProvider(kind, factory) into a module-level Map, resolveProvider(kind, settings, deps) -> BoxProvider or throws box_pool_provider_unavailable, clearBoxProviderRegistry() for test isolation.

FakeBoxProvider (kind 'fake'): in-memory Map keyed by boxId, provision() returns synthetic workerHost 'fake://box-<boxId>' idempotent on boxId, probe() ok unless test injects failure, destroy() idempotent, list() returns the set, AND a probe-byte-counter so a test can assert ZERO fs I/O. capabilities { sshAddressable:false, ephemeral:false, usesLedger:false }. Deterministic via injected ClockPort. Failure-injection hooks.

StaticSshBoxProvider (kind 'static-ssh'): provision() hands out fixed addresses from settings.providerOptions sshHosts idempotently (min==max==len); reads BOTH snake_case ssh_hosts AND camelCase sshHosts from providerOptions (config passes providerOptions through un-normalized). destroy() is a no-op that just forgets the host (never deletes the machine, runs NO hooks). probe() runs runSsh(host,'printf ready',{timeoutMs: worker.sshTimeoutMs}). list() returns the configured set. capabilities { sshAddressable:true, ephemeral:false, usesLedger:false }.

runProviderConformanceSuite(makeProvider, opts): shared parameterized test body asserting the BoxProvider contract (idempotent provision on boxId, idempotent destroy, list reflects provisioned-minus-destroyed, probe gating). Imported by fake/static-ssh tests and by each cloud provider's gated test.
Mutex unit tests: serializes concurrent runExclusive calls, releases on throw.
BoxPool unit tests including the concurrent-growth-cannot-exceed-max reservation test and reaper-vs-release race test.
BoxLease unit tests: release/fail/heartbeat, settled double-settle no-op, leaseId cross-generation stale no-op (does not touch inFlight), release-after-DESTROYED no-op, healthy-vs-poison distinction.
Reaper unit tests over a fake ClockPort including orphan-vs-alive distinction and the reaper-clears-lease-table-and-inFlight-on-orphan-destroy assertion.

Ledger unit tests: write-ahead-before-provision, upsert-after-provision correlate, replay on hydrate, atomic write, corruption tolerance, disabled for non-cloud, daily-spend sidecar persists and resets across day boundary.
FakeBoxProvider conformance suite + failure-injection + zero-fs-I/O assertion.

StaticSshBoxProvider conformance suite via installEvalSsh (always-on, zero daemon); asserts provider reads BOTH ssh_hosts (snake) and sshHosts (camel) from providerOptions; probe uses worker.sshTimeoutMs.

fast-check v4 (^4.8.0, matches root package.json) property suite: inventory in [min..max], inFlight>=0, monotonic box-seconds, ledger-disabled providers do zero fs I/O, leaseId stale-release always no-op, LEASED never destroyed, two concurrent growth ops never exceed max.
Reusable installEvalSsh helper extracted/adapted from workspace-prompt-resume.test.ts so static-ssh + live tests share one eval-ssh transport shim.
Always-on end-to-end demo (T19): real runDaemon path with tracker.kind=memory + provider=fake.
Loopback + BYO/docker live test gated by SYMPHONY_TS_RUN_LIVE_SSH_E2E=1, reusing setupNativeSshdWorker (live-ssh.test.ts:195).

publicInterfaces.name: BoxProvider
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxProvider { readonly kind: BoxPoolProvider; provision(req: ProvisionRequest): Promise<BoxDescriptor>; probe(box: BoxDescriptor, opts: { timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<BoxHealth>; destroy(box: BoxDescriptor, opts: { timeoutMs: number; reason: TeardownReason }): Promise<void>; list(): Promise<BoxDescriptor[]>; readonly capabilities: ProviderCapabilities; }

publicInterfaces.name: ProvisionRequest
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface ProvisionRequest { boxId: string; affinityKey?: string | null; labels: ReadonlyArray<string>; timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal; providerOptions?: Record<string, unknown>; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxDescriptor
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxDescriptor { boxId: string; workerHost: string; providerRef: string; createdAtMs: number; labels: ReadonlyArray<string>; metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxHealth
publicInterfaces.signature: export type BoxHealth = { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: string };

publicInterfaces.name: ProviderCapabilities
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface ProviderCapabilities { sshAddressable: boolean; ephemeral: boolean; usesLedger: boolean; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxPool
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxPool { acquire(req: AcquireRequest): Promise<AcquireResult>; canAcquire(): boolean; reconcile(next: BoxPoolSettings): void; hydrate(): Promise<void>; drain(opts: { deadlineMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<void>; snapshot(): BoxPoolSnapshot; }

publicInterfaces.name: AcquireRequest
publicInterfaces.signature: export type AcquireRequest = { issueId: string; slotIndex: number; labels: ReadonlyArray<string>; affinityKey?: string | null; timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal };

publicInterfaces.name: AcquireResult
publicInterfaces.signature: export type AcquireResult = { status: 'leased'; lease: BoxLease } | { status: 'no_capacity'; reason: 'acquire_timeout' | 'spend_cap' | 'pool_disabled' | 'provider_error' };

publicInterfaces.name: BoxLease
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxLease { readonly leaseId: string; readonly boxId: string; readonly workerHost: string; readonly acquiredAtMs: number; readonly expiresAtMs: number | null; release(outcome?: BoxOutcome): Promise<void>; fail(reason: string): Promise<void>; heartbeat(): void; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxOutcome
publicInterfaces.signature: export type BoxOutcome = 'healthy' | 'poison'; // release(outcome) defaults 'healthy'; fail(reason) is equivalent to release('poison') with a recorded reason

publicInterfaces.name: WarmupStrategy (internal, NOT hook-running)
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface WarmupStrategy { // box-readiness only; NEVER runs workspace hooks (runner owns those) ensureReady(box: BoxDescriptor, provider: BoxProvider, opts: { timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<BoxHealth>; }

publicInterfaces.name: TeardownReason
publicInterfaces.signature: export type TeardownReason = 'ttl' | 'idle' | 'shrink' | 'unhealthy' | 'failed' | 'drain' | 'orphan';

publicInterfaces.name: BoxState (internal)
publicInterfaces.signature: type BoxState = 'PROVISIONING' | 'WARMING' | 'WARM_IDLE' | 'LEASED' | 'RETURNING' | 'DEGRADED' | 'DESTROYING' | 'DESTROYED' | 'DRAINING';

publicInterfaces.name: createBoxPool (factory)
publicInterfaces.signature: export function createBoxPool(settings: BoxPoolSettings, deps: { clock: ClockPort; logEvent: (e: Record<string, unknown>) => void; ledgerPath?: string }): BoxPool; // NOTE: no runHook/createWorkspaceForIssue deps - the pool never touches workspaces

publicInterfaces.name: registerBoxProvider / resolveProvider / clearBoxProviderRegistry
publicInterfaces.signature: export type BoxProviderFactory = (settings: BoxPoolSettings, deps: ProviderDeps) => BoxProvider; export function registerBoxProvider(kind: BoxPoolProvider, factory: BoxProviderFactory): void; export function resolveProvider(kind: BoxPoolProvider, settings: BoxPoolSettings, deps: ProviderDeps): BoxProvider; export function clearBoxProviderRegistry(): void;

publicInterfaces.name: ProviderDeps
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface ProviderDeps { clock: ClockPort; logEvent: (e: Record<string, unknown>) => void; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxPoolSnapshot
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxPoolSnapshot { enabled: boolean; provider: BoxPoolProvider; total: number; warmIdle: number; leased: number; provisioning: number; degraded: number; inFlight: number; spend: { concurrentBoxes: number; boxSecondsUsed: number; dailyBoxSecondsUsed: number; dayKey: string }; boxes: ReadonlyArray<{ boxId: string; workerHost: string; state: BoxState; inFlight: number; markedForDestroy: boolean }>; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxPoolSettings (domain)
publicInterfaces.signature: export interface BoxPoolSettings { enabled: boolean; provider: BoxPoolProvider; min: number; max: number; warm: number; maxInFlight: number; ttlMs: number; idleReapMs: number; acquireTimeoutMs: number; reapIntervalMs: number; staleHeartbeatMs: number; drainDeadlineMs: number; maxBoxesPerIssue?: number | undefined; spend?: { maxConcurrentBoxes?: number; maxBoxSeconds?: number; dailyBoxSeconds?: number }; providerOptions?: Record<string, unknown>; }

publicInterfaces.name: BoxPoolProvider (domain)
publicInterfaces.signature: export const PROVIDER_KINDS = ['fake', 'static-ssh', 'cloud'] as const; export type BoxPoolProvider = (typeof PROVIDER_KINDS)[number];

publicInterfaces.name: SymphonyRuntimeOptions.boxPool (runtime injection)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface SymphonyRuntimeOptions { /* ...existing... */ boxPool?: BoxPool | undefined; }

publicInterfaces.name: SymphonyRuntime.drainBoxPool (new async, awaited by main.ts after start() returns)
publicInterfaces.signature: async drainBoxPool(): Promise<void>; // calls this.input.boxPool?.drain({ deadlineMs: settings.worker.boxPool.drainDeadlineMs }) once; idempotent

publicInterfaces.name: Orchestrator capacity probe + abandonClaim + affinity
publicInterfaces.signature: new Orchestrator(settings, clock?, state?, capacityProbe?: { canAcquire(): boolean } | undefined); // canAcquire consulted by workerCapacityAvailable() when present; claim() sets RunningEntry.workerHost to a 'pending://<issueId>/<slot>' sentinel and RunningEntry.affinityHost to retry?.workerHost when probe present

publicInterfaces.name: Orchestrator.abandonClaim (new, inverse of claim - NO retry record)
publicInterfaces.signature: abandonClaim(issueId: string, slotIndex: number): void; // deletes running + claimed for the slot WITHOUT adding a retry/backoff, so next poll re-evaluates capacity via canAcquire()

publicInterfaces.name: Orchestrator.setWorkerHost (new write-back setter)
publicInterfaces.signature: setWorkerHost(issueId: string, slotIndex: number, host: string): void; // overwrites the pending sentinel with the real lease workerHost before the runner call

publicInterfaces.name: RunningEntry.affinityHost (new domain field)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface RunningEntry { /* ...existing... */ affinityHost?: string | null | undefined; } // carries retry workerHost across the bypass so affinity is NOT lost to the sentinel

configAdditions:
DOMAIN (packages/domain/src/index.ts):
- Near TRACKER_KINDS (:15) add: `export const PROVIDER_KINDS = ['fake','static-ssh','cloud'] as const; export type BoxPoolProvider = (typeof PROVIDER_KINDS)[number];` mirroring the as-const + derived-type pattern.
- Add `export interface BoxPoolSettings { ... }` (full shape in publicInterfaces) with JSDoc; durations in ms, spend in seconds (matching UsageTotals.secondsRunning).
- Add `boxPool?: BoxPoolSettings | undefined;` to WorkerSettings (164-178) with JSDoc.
- Add `affinityHost?: string | null | undefined;` to RunningEntry (so retry workerHost survives the bypass). NON-BREAKING optional field.

CONFIG (packages/config/src/index.ts):
- Extend workerRawSchema (71-77): add `boxPool: z.unknown().optional()`; define a separate `.strict()` boxPoolRawSchema with keys (enabled, provider, min, max, warm, maxInFlight, ttlMs, idleReapMs, acquireTimeoutMs, reapIntervalMs, staleHeartbeatMs, drainDeadlineMs, maxBoxesPerIssue, spend, providerOptions) each z.unknown().optional(); validate worker.boxPool against it inside parseBoxPool. spend is a nested .strict() schema (maxConcurrentBoxes, maxBoxSeconds, dailyBoxSeconds); providerOptions is rawRecordSchema (passthrough map, NO key validation).
- Aliases: `boxPoolAliases = { max_in_flight:'maxInFlight', ttl_ms:'ttlMs', idle_reap_ms:'idleReapMs', acquire_timeout_ms:'acquireTimeoutMs', reap_interval_ms:'reapIntervalMs', stale_heartbeat_ms:'staleHeartbeatMs', drain_deadline_ms:'drainDeadlineMs', max_boxes_per_issue:'maxBoxesPerIssue', provider_options:'providerOptions' }` and `boxPoolSpendAliases = { max_concurrent_boxes:'maxConcurrentBoxes', max_box_seconds:'maxBoxSeconds', daily_box_seconds:'dailyBoxSeconds' }`. Add `box_pool:'boxPool'` to workerAliases. In normalizeWorkflowConfig after `normalizeNested(normalized,'worker',workerAliases)`: if isPlainRecord(normalized.worker) && isPlainRecord(normalized.worker.boxPool) { normalizeNested(normalized.worker,'boxPool',boxPoolAliases); if isPlainRecord(boxPool.spend) normalizeNested(boxPool,'spend',boxPoolSpendAliases); }. CRITICAL: providerOptions is NOT alias-normalized (the normalizer is a flat per-key map, verified normalizeNested does not recurse). Therefore the static-ssh provider MUST accept both `ssh_hosts` and `sshHosts` from providerOptions (handled in the provider, T13), and this is documented next to boxPoolAliases as an intentional passthrough.
- `function parseBoxPool(raw): BoxPoolSettings | undefined`: returns undefined when raw is undefined; else coerce: enabled via booleanValue(...,false); provider via boxPoolProviderValue(...,'fake','worker.box_pool.provider') validating against PROVIDER_KINDS (throws `unsupported worker.box_pool.provider: <v>`); min/warm via nonNegativeIntWithFallback (min=0, warm=1); max via positiveInt(...,1,'worker.box_pool.max'); maxInFlight via positiveInt(...,1,'worker.box_pool.max_in_flight'); ttlMs positiveInt(...,3_600_000); idleReapMs positiveInt(...,300_000); acquireTimeoutMs positiveInt(...,30_000); reapIntervalMs positiveInt(...,15_000); staleHeartbeatMs positiveInt(...,600_000); drainDeadlineMs positiveInt(...,30_000,'worker.box_pool.drain_deadline_ms'); maxBoxesPerIssue positiveInt only when present; spend.* via positiveInt (seconds) only when present; providerOptions via optionalMap. Cross-field guards: throw `worker.box_pool.max must be >= worker.box_pool.min`; `worker.box_pool.warm must be <= worker.box_pool.max`; if enabled and provider==='static-ssh' require providerOptions.ssh_hosts/sshHosts to be a non-empty string array else throw `worker.box_pool.provider_options.ssh_hosts is required for static-ssh`.
- parseConfig worker block (334-348): `settings.worker.boxPool = parseBoxPool(workerRaw.boxPool);`. Anti-double-capacity guard: `if (settings.worker.boxPool?.enabled && settings.worker.sshHosts.length > 0) throw new Error('worker.box_pool.enabled cannot be combined with worker.ssh_hosts');`.
- defaultSettings (283): leave `worker: { sshHosts: [], sshTimeoutMs: 60_000 }` UNCHANGED (no boxPool key).
- cloneSettings: change worker clone to deep-copy boxPool INCLUDING nested array/object contents of providerOptions and spend. VERIFIED gap: a shallow `{...providerOptions}` aliases the inner ssh_hosts array across the hot per-issue settingsForIssueState clone. Use `boxPool: settings.worker.boxPool ? { ...settings.worker.boxPool, spend: settings.worker.boxPool.spend ? { ...settings.worker.boxPool.spend } : undefined, providerOptions: settings.worker.boxPool.providerOptions ? deepMerge({}, settings.worker.boxPool.providerOptions) : undefined } : undefined` (deepMerge already exists in config and recursively clones nested records/arrays-by-reference -> wrap arrays specifically: providerOptions cloned via a structuredClone fallback to guarantee inner arrays are copied).
- Provider-availability validation stays OUT of config: config enforces enum + numeric + cross-field + static-ssh ssh_hosts presence only. Registry/credential checks happen at daemon construction (resolveProvider throws box_pool_provider_unavailable at startup).

WORKFLOW.md FRONT MATTER:
- No change required for backward compat (boxPool absent => disabled). Documented opt-in snake_case shape under `worker:`:
  worker:
    box_pool:
      enabled: true
      provider: static-ssh        # fake | static-ssh | cloud
      min: 0
      max: 4
      warm: 1
      max_in_flight: 1            # only 1 is safe vs the workerHost-keyed MCP tunnel refCount
      ttl_ms: 3600000
      idle_reap_ms: 300000
      acquire_timeout_ms: 30000
      reap_interval_ms: 15000
      stale_heartbeat_ms: 600000
      drain_deadline_ms: 30000
      max_boxes_per_issue: 2      # optional fairness cap so one ensemble cannot monopolize the pool
      spend:
        max_concurrent_boxes: 4
        max_box_seconds: 7200      # seconds
        daily_box_seconds: 86400   # seconds, UTC-day rolling; persisted across restart via spend.json sidecar
      provider_options:
        ssh_hosts: ["user@host-a:22", "user@host-b:22"]   # static-ssh; accepted as ssh_hosts OR sshHosts
  (when box_pool.enabled is true, worker.ssh_hosts MUST be empty - rejected at parse time).

DEFAULTS SUMMARY: enabled=false (absent), provider='fake', min=0, max=1, warm=1, maxInFlight=1, ttlMs=3_600_000, idleReapMs=300_000, acquireTimeoutMs=30_000, reapIntervalMs=15_000, staleHeartbeatMs=600_000, drainDeadlineMs=30_000, maxBoxesPerIssue undefined (uncapped), spend undefined (uncapped), providerOptions undefined.

integrationEdits.change: Add PROVIDER_KINDS const + BoxPoolProvider type near :15; add BoxPoolSettings interface; add boxPool?: to WorkerSettings (164-178); add affinityHost?: string|null to RunningEntry. All additive/optional - no behavior change.

Extend workerRawSchema with boxPool; add boxPoolRawSchema + spend sub-schema; add box_pool/boxPool aliases + spend aliases + normalizeNested wiring (NOT into providerOptions); add parseBoxPool() + boxPoolProviderValue(); call settings.worker.boxPool = parseBoxPool(...) in parseConfig; add anti-double-capacity guard + static-ssh ssh_hosts presence guard; extend cloneSettings to DEEP-copy boxPool incl. nested providerOptions arrays (structuredClone fallback) + spend. defaultSettings unchanged. Add a regression: a deep-equality snapshot test (T2) pins that absent box_pool yields a Settings clone byte-identical to the pre-change clone.

(1) Add optional 4th ctor param capacityProbe?: { canAcquire(): boolean } stored on a private field. (2) workerCapacityAvailable() (163-166): if capacityProbe present return capacityProbe.canAcquire(); else keep sshHosts logic. (3) claim() (116-117): when capacityProbe present, BYPASS selectWorkerHost - set entry.workerHost = `pending://${issue.id}/${slotIndex}` sentinel (NOT null, so no projection ever shows 'local') AND entry.affinityHost = retry?.workerHost ?? null (so retry affinity survives the bypass - VERIFIED retry.workerHost at :218). Only bypass when probe present; static path unchanged. (4) Add setWorkerHost(issueId, slotIndex, host) overwriting the running entry's workerHost. (5) Add abandonClaim(issueId, slotIndex): delete running+claimed for that slot with NO retry record (inverse of claim; distinct from finish() which schedules backoff at :207-221, and from cleanupIssue() which marks completed). Used by runtime on claim-time no_capacity so next poll re-evaluates via canAcquire().

(1) Add boxPool?: BoxPool to SymphonyRuntimeOptions. (2) Construct Orchestrator with capacityProbe = input.boxPool ? { canAcquire: () => input.boxPool!.canAcquire() } : undefined. (3) maybeDispatch passes the new claim.affinityHost into runClaim. (4) In runClaim, BEFORE this.runner: if this.input.boxPool, read affinity from the RunningEntry.affinityHost (NOT claim.workerHost which is the pending sentinel), call boxPool.acquire({ issueId, slotIndex, labels: ensembleLabels(issue), affinityKey: affinityHost, timeoutMs: settings.worker.boxPool.acquireTimeoutMs, signal: handle.signal }). If no_capacity: addEvent('dispatch_skipped', `${issue.identifier} worker_host_capacity`), call this.orchestrator.abandonClaim(issue.id, slotIndex) (NOT finish - avoids backoff churn), handle.release(), return without running and without recordHistory. If leased: effectiveWorkerHost = lease.workerHost; this.orchestrator.setWorkerHost(issue.id, slotIndex, effectiveWorkerHost) BEFORE runner so the snapshot/history (entry?.workerHost at :462,:497) sees the real address; pass effectiveWorkerHost into runner. Wrap onUpdate to also call lease.heartbeat(). (5) Track a boxOutcome flag local to runClaim: default 'healthy'; in the catch, set it from a TYPED classifier classifyBoxOutcome(error) that returns 'poison' ONLY for box-transport faults (ssh_timeout, remote_home_lookup_failed, workspace_prepare_failed, workspace hook failed over ssh) and 'healthy' for local faults (ssh_not_found local ENOENT, invalid_ssh_timeout config, agent_run_aborted, normal agent failures). The classifier matches typed prefixes, not arbitrary substrings. (6) In the finally (509-511): keep handle.release(); then if a lease was acquired: boxOutcome==='poison' ? await lease.fail(reason) : await lease.release('healthy'). Lease ops are leaseId+settled+state-guarded so a stale generation is a no-op.

reconcileStalledRuns -> finishExternally() aborts but does NOT release a lease (VERIFIED). The lease is owned by the still-running runClaim whose finally will eventually release it AFTER session.stop()/afterRun (agent-runner:179-194) drain or time out over sshTimeoutMs. To bound that and prevent a held-lease leak: when reconcileStalledRuns finishes a run externally for a boxPool-enabled run, mark the box poisoned by recording the stall reason against the lease via a new boxPool.markStalled(issueId, slotIndex) advisory call OR rely on the leaseId-guarded fail in runClaim's catch (the abort makes runner throw agent_run_aborted -> classifier returns 'healthy', so the box is released not failed). To avoid recycling-as-healthy a box that hosted a stall, classifyBoxOutcome treats a run finished while handle was aborted-due-to-stall as 'poison'. Implementation: runClaim checks `!handle.isActive` (already at :474) and, if the run was force-finished externally (a flag set by finishExternally via a small handle.reason field), classifies 'poison'. A test asserts the lease is failed (not released) when a run is stall-finished, and that inFlight/spend accounting is correct afterward.

In reloadWorkflowIfConfigured (519-532): after this.orchestrator.settings = workflow.settings, add `if (this.input.boxPool && workflow.settings.worker.boxPool) this.input.boxPool.reconcile(workflow.settings.worker.boxPool);`. ALSO: when a reload throws because the anti-double-capacity guard fired, the existing catch keeps last-good and only emits workflow_reload_failed; add the guard's specific message into that event so operators see WHY the pool did not turn on (addEvent already includes errorMessage(error)).

DO NOT make stop() async (it is synchronous void at :301-307 and start() relies on it only flipping this.stopped). Instead add async drainBoxPool() that awaits this.input.boxPool?.drain({ deadlineMs: settings.worker.boxPool?.drainDeadlineMs ?? 30000 }) once (idempotent guard). stop() stays sync and additionally aborts in-flight runner handles (already does). drainBoxPool is invoked by main.ts AFTER runtime.start() resolves (start() returns once stopped). drain() flips DRAINING, rejects new acquires, waits inFlight to the deadline, then aborts remaining handles and force-destroys all boxes.

Add a buildBoxPool(settings, env) helper: when settings.worker.boxPool?.enabled, import { createBoxPool, registerBoxProvider } from '@symphony/worker-box-pool' (built-in fake + static-ssh self-register on import), construct boxPool = createBoxPool(settings.worker.boxPool, { clock: systemClock, logEvent: (e) => void appendLogEvent(settings.logging.logFile, e), ledgerPath: path.join(<workspace.root>/.symphony/box-pool, 'ledger.json') }). resolveProvider throws box_pool_provider_unavailable here for an unregistered/credential-missing enabled kind (fail loud at startup). Return undefined when disabled.

In runDaemon (100-170): build boxPool only when enabled, pass `boxPool` into new SymphonyRuntime({...}). After construction and before start, call `await boxPool?.hydrate()` to re-adopt boxes. Keep the sync `const stop = () => runtime.stop()` SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers. In the existing try/finally around runtime.start (159-164): after `await runtime.start(...)` resolves (i.e. after stop flips stopped), in the finally call `await runtime.drainBoxPool();` BEFORE server?.stop() so paid cloud boxes are destroyed before exit. When boxPool undefined, drainBoxPool is a no-op and behavior is byte-identical.

Add re-exports for live/e2e test imports: export { createBoxPool, registerBoxProvider, resolveProvider, clearBoxProviderRegistry, FakeBoxProvider, StaticSshBoxProvider } from '@symphony/worker-box-pool'; export type { BoxPool, BoxLease, BoxProvider, BoxPoolSnapshot, AcquireResult } from '@symphony/worker-box-pool'. Add @symphony/worker-box-pool to apps/cli/package.json deps.

AUDIT readers of RunningEntry.workerHost. VERIFIED: runtimeRunningEntry (runtime:846) and recordHistory (runtime:462,497) read entry.workerHost from the orchestrator snapshot AFTER setWorkerHost overwrites the sentinel. The pending sentinel `pending://...` is observable only in the window between claim() and acquire() (emit() at maybeDispatch:400). Mitigation (concrete, replaces the open question): use a `pending://` sentinel rather than null so the TUI never renders a misleading 'local'; a runtime test asserts a snapshot taken in that window shows the sentinel and the post-acquire snapshot shows the real host, and history.workerHost == lease.workerHost. Likely zero projection code change.
Add packages/worker-box-pool to TS project references and the workspace packages glob (mirror worker-host-pool).

tasks.title: Scaffold @symphony/worker-box-pool + mutex
tasks.detail: Create package.json (deps: domain, ports, ssh; NO workspace dep), tsconfig.json, src/index.ts (empty barrel), src/types.ts with ALL types incl. BoxOutcome and Mutex, src/mutex.ts + test/mutex.test.ts (TDD: serializes concurrent runExclusive, releases lock on throw). Add to root tsconfig references + pnpm workspace. Verify `pnpm -C ts build` compiles.

tasks.title: Domain types: PROVIDER_KINDS, BoxPoolSettings, WorkerSettings.boxPool, RunningEntry.affinityHost
tasks.detail: TDD: extend packages/domain test asserting PROVIDER_KINDS membership, BoxPoolSettings shape compiles, RunningEntry.affinityHost is optional. Add const+type near :15, BoxPoolSettings interface, boxPool?: on WorkerSettings, affinityHost?: on RunningEntry. No runtime behavior.

tasks.title: Config: parseBoxPool + aliases + deep cloneSettings + guards + byte-identical regression
tasks.detail: TDD first: (a) parse worker.box_pool, assert all coercions/defaults/snake-case aliasing/cross-field guards (max>=min, warm<=max)/provider enum/static-ssh ssh_hosts-required/enabled+sshHosts rejection; (b) cloneSettings deep-copies boxPool+spend+providerOptions INCLUDING nested ssh_hosts array (mutate clone's providerOptions.ssh_hosts[0], assert original array element unchanged); (c) absent box_pool leaves settings.worker.boxPool undefined; (d) REGRESSION: deep-equality snapshot that an absent-box_pool Settings clone equals the pre-change clone shape (guards byte-identical claim). Then implement boxPoolRawSchema, boxPoolProviderValue, parseBoxPool, alias wiring (NOT into providerOptions), parseConfig call, guards, deep cloneSettings.

tasks.title: Ledger: write-ahead + post-provision correlate + daily-spend sidecar
tasks.detail: TDD: provisional row flushed BEFORE provision; UPSERT with providerRef/workerHost after provision returns (covers crash between create and response - recoverable by labels OR provisional boxId); load/replay; atomic tmp+rename; corruption tolerance (returns [] defers to list()); upsert/delete; daily-spend sidecar spend.json persists boxSecondsToday+dayKey and resets on UTC day-key change. Implement src/ledger.ts. Pure fs over injected path; no pool coupling.

tasks.title: Registry: register/resolve/clear
tasks.detail: TDD: register a stub factory, resolve it, assert box_pool_provider_unavailable for unregistered kind, clearBoxProviderRegistry isolates tests. Implement src/registry.ts (module-level Map). No built-ins yet.

tasks.title: Provider conformance suite (shared test body)
tasks.detail: Implement src/conformance.ts exporting runProviderConformanceSuite(makeProvider, opts): provision idempotent on boxId, destroy idempotent (already-gone ok), list() reflects provisioned-minus-destroyed, probe gates (created-but-unreachable -> ok:false). No test file of its own.

tasks.title: FakeBoxProvider + zero-I/O assertion
tasks.detail: TDD in test/providers/fake.test.ts: run conformance suite + failure injection (probe/provision/destroy) + assert ZERO fs writes (probe a write-counter). Implement src/providers/fake.ts: Map<boxId>, synthetic fake://box-<boxId>, deterministic ClockPort, capabilities usesLedger:false.

tasks.title: Lease: settled + leaseId ownership + healthy/poison + mutex-coordinated settle
tasks.detail: TDD in test/lease.test.ts: release('healthy') keeps box; fail(reason) marks poison; second settle is a no-op (settled flag); stale leaseId (record.leaseId mismatch) is a no-op that does NOT touch inFlight (cross-generation); release after box DESTROYED is a no-op; heartbeat updates lastHeartbeatMs. Implement src/lease.ts: pure factory over BoxRecord + injected onSettle callback that runs inside the per-box mutex (so reaper and release cannot both decrement inFlight). Depends on types+mutex only.

tasks.title: BoxPool core: inventory, acquire/canAcquire, RESERVATION-based single-flight growth, FIFO waiter, spend, maxBoxesPerIssue
tasks.detail: TDD in test/pool.test.ts (FakeBoxProvider + fake ClockPort): acquire leased when warm; grows under max; blocks to acquireTimeoutMs then no_capacity:acquire_timeout (abortable via signal -> no_capacity); pool_disabled when enabled:false; spend caps (concurrent boxes, box-seconds, dailyBoxSeconds) return no_capacity:spend_cap BEFORE leasing AND warming respects maxConcurrentBoxes; canAcquire conservative; sticky affinityKey reuses same boxId; synchronous select-and-stamp (no await between select WARM_IDLE, stamp leaseId, ++inFlight); TWO CONCURRENT GROWTH ops cannot exceed max - reservation counter incremented synchronously before the provision await and decremented on settle/reject (this replaces boxId-keyed single-flight which CANNOT dedupe distinct boxIds); maxInFlight>1 allows N concurrent leases per box, N+1 blocks; maxBoxesPerIssue caps boxes one issue's slots can hold so an ensemble cannot monopolize the pool (waiter blocked when issue is at its per-issue cap even if global capacity exists). Implement src/pool.ts (lease.ts, registry.ts, ledger when usesLedger, per-box mutex). Reaper hook is a stub; body in T9.

tasks.title: Reaper: ttl/idle/orphan/list-reconcile/top-up + re-entrancy + unref + reaper-vs-release race
tasks.detail: TDD in test/reaper.test.ts (fake ClockPort): ttl/idle reaping respects min floor; LEASED past ttl only markedForDestroy, recycled when inFlight->0 INSIDE the per-box mutex; DEGRADED on probe fail; ORPHAN distinction - a stale-heartbeat lease whose run is GONE is force-returned (clears lease table AND decrements inFlight) but a long-but-alive single turn (no onUpdate yet beyond staleHeartbeatMs) is NOT destroyed when an aliveProbe/active-handle check says it is still running; list() authoritative - labeled pool-owned-but-unknown destroyed, UNLABELED never destroyed, registered-but-missing marked DESTROYED; top-up toward min/warm only within budget; serial in-progress guard (reaper-vs-reaper); REAPER-VS-RELEASE: a release and a reaper tick both observing inFlight->0 are serialized by the per-box mutex so only one destroy happens and inFlight never underflows; ASSERT pool calls timerHandle.unref?.() on the reaper timer (since systemClock never unrefs). Implement src/reaper.ts + wire pool start/stop/unref.

tasks.title: BoxPool reconcile / hydrate / drain (awaitable, leak-free)
tasks.detail: TDD in test/pool.test.ts (extend): reconcile diffs prev-vs-next (resize toward min/max, defer shrink to reaper oldest-idle-first, never reconstruct, never destroy leased synchronously, enabled false->true grow, true->false drain-to-zero, raising min/warm tops up); hydrate replays ledger + provider.list() + re-adopts boxes + force-returns orphan leased rows whose run is gone; drain({deadlineMs,signal}) flips DRAINING, rejects new acquires (returns no_capacity:pool_disabled), waits inFlight to deadline then aborts + force-destroys ALL boxes (assert zero boxes remain after drain even when a lease was held - the leak fix); drain is idempotent and resolves (so main.ts can await it). Implement methods in src/pool.ts.

tasks.title: createBoxPool factory + register built-ins + barrel
tasks.detail: TDD: createBoxPool wires deps (clock, logEvent, ledgerPath) WITHOUT any workspace/hook deps; registers fake + static-ssh on import of index.ts; throws box_pool_provider_unavailable when enabled kind unregistered. Implement src/index.ts barrel + factory glue (static-ssh registered in T12).

tasks.title: StaticSshBoxProvider + eval-ssh conformance (snake+camel ssh_hosts)
tasks.detail: TDD in test/providers/static-ssh.test.ts via installEvalSsh (extract reusable helper into src/test-support/evalSsh.ts adapted from workspace-prompt-resume.test.ts:668), zero daemon: run conformance suite over eval-ssh; ASSERT provider reads ssh_hosts (snake) AND sshHosts (camel) from providerOptions (the documented passthrough); probe runs runSsh printf-ready with worker.sshTimeoutMs; provision hands out hosts idempotently (min==max==len); destroy forgets host, runs NO hooks, never deletes machine. Implement src/providers/static-ssh.ts.

tasks.title: Property tests (fast-check ^4.8.0)
tasks.detail: Add test/pool-props.test.ts (sibling to policies/test/workerHost-props.test.ts) with arbitraries over sequences of acquire/release/fail/reconcile/reap: inventory in [min..max] (min respected unless draining), inFlight>=0, monotonic box-seconds per box, ledger-disabled providers do zero fs writes, leaseId stale-release always no-op, LEASED never destroyed by reaper, AND two concurrent growth decisions never exceed max (reservation invariant).

tasks.title: Orchestrator: capacityProbe + sentinel bypass (affinity preserved) + abandonClaim + setWorkerHost
tasks.detail: TDD in packages/orchestrator/test: with capacityProbe present, workerCapacityAvailable() returns probe.canAcquire() and selectWorkerHost is bypassed - entry.workerHost is the `pending://${id}/${slot}` SENTINEL (never null) and entry.affinityHost == retry?.workerHost (assert retry affinity is preserved, NOT lost); without probe, sshHosts behavior unchanged; setWorkerHost overwrites the sentinel; abandonClaim deletes running+claimed for the slot with NO retry record (assert retryAttempts unchanged and the slot is immediately re-claimable next tick, distinct from finish() which adds backoff). Implement the 4th ctor param + claim bypass + setWorkerHost + abandonClaim.

tasks.title: Runtime: acquire-before-run, structured release/fail, abandonClaim on no_capacity, heartbeat, reconcile-on-reload, drainBoxPool
tasks.detail: TDD in packages/runtime/test with a FAKE BoxPool injected: (a) acquire called with affinityKey == prior workerHost on retry (uses RunningEntry.affinityHost, NOT the sentinel - assert affinityKey is the real prior host); (b) no_capacity -> runner NOT called, orchestrator.abandonClaim invoked (NOT finish), handle released, NO history recorded, NO retry/backoff scheduled, next poll re-evaluates; (c) leased.workerHost written back via setWorkerHost before runner and passed to runner; (d) history.workerHost == lease.workerHost; (e) success -> lease.release('healthy') in finally; (f) classifier: ssh_timeout/workspace_prepare_failed -> lease.fail (poison); ssh_not_found(local ENOENT)/invalid_ssh_timeout/agent_run_aborted/ordinary agent failure -> lease.release('healthy') (NEGATIVE test that a healthy box is NOT recycled on a local error); (g) stale-generation late resolve -> lease op is a no-op (leaseId guard); (h) onUpdate triggers lease.heartbeat; (i) stall-finished run (finishExternally) -> lease eventually FAILED (poison) not released, inFlight/spend correct afterward; (j) reload calls boxPool.reconcile; (k) reload that throws the anti-double-capacity guard keeps last-good AND the workflow_reload_failed event includes the guard message; (l) drainBoxPool awaits boxPool.drain; (m) boxPool undefined => byte-identical regression (acquire never called, classifier never runs). Implement runtime edits + Orchestrator construction + classifyBoxOutcome + drainBoxPool.

tasks.title: Daemon + CLI wiring + re-exports + awaited drain on exit
tasks.detail: TDD where feasible (apps/cli or daemon unit): when worker.boxPool.enabled, buildBoxPool constructs the pool, registers providers, injects into runtime, hydrate() on startup, and drainBoxPool() is AWAITED in main.ts's finally after start() returns (assert drain resolves before server.stop / process exit); when disabled, boxPool undefined and behavior byte-identical. resolveProvider failure -> box_pool_provider_unavailable at startup. Implement daemon.ts helper, main.ts wiring (sync stop handlers + awaited drainBoxPool in finally), apps/cli/src/index.ts re-exports, add dep to apps/cli/package.json.

tasks.title: E2E demo (always-on): MemoryTracker + fake-pool through real runDaemon
tasks.detail: Add ts/test/box-pool-e2e-memory.test.ts (always-on): real runDaemon path with tracker.kind=memory (memoryIssuesFromEnv), worker.box_pool.enabled provider=fake max=1 warm=1, PATH-shimmed fake executor. Assert: issue claimed, pool leases fake box, RunningEntry/history.workerHost == fake://box-<id>, run completes, lease released, snapshot shows warmIdle returned; then fill the single box at maxInFlight (or max=0-effective) and assert the blocked issue surfaces worker_host_capacity (via canAcquire at eligibility) with NO claim-then-backoff churn. Also assert SIGINT path: trigger stop, await drainBoxPool, snapshot shows zero boxes.

tasks.title: Live SSH test (env-gated): loopback + BYO/docker + retry affinity + drain leak
tasks.detail: Add ts/test/box-pool-live-ssh.test.ts gated by SYMPHONY_TS_RUN_LIVE_SSH_E2E=1 reusing setupNativeSshdWorker (live-ssh.test.ts:195): real acquire over loopback sshd -> assert real workspace created by the RUNNER (not the pool) over SSH -> run -> release; fail() force-recycles a poisoned box; probe via runSsh printf-ready; END-TO-END RETRY AFFINITY: a retry re-leases the SAME boxId and resume state is preserved (asserts affinity is not vacuously null); drain force-destroys remaining boxes (no leak). Multi-box + per-box isolation + sticky-reacquire-across-retry behind SYMPHONY_LIVE_SSH_WORKER_HOSTS. Collected-but-skipped without the gate.

fail(reason) marks box poison for recycle
second settle on same lease is a no-op (settled flag), inFlight unchanged
stale leaseId release is a no-op and does NOT touch inFlight (cross-generation)
stale leaseId fail is a no-op
release after box DESTROYED is a no-op
heartbeat updates lastHeartbeatMs
settle runs inside the per-box mutex (no concurrent decrement)
acquire leased when warm box free
acquire grows under max when no warm box
acquire blocks to acquireTimeoutMs then no_capacity:acquire_timeout
abort signal resolves acquire to no_capacity promptly (does not block poll thread)
no_capacity:pool_disabled when enabled false
no_capacity:spend_cap when maxConcurrentBoxes reached BEFORE leasing
no_capacity:spend_cap when dailyBoxSeconds exhausted
warming respects maxConcurrentBoxes (pre-provision cannot exceed cap)
canAcquire false when full and cannot grow; true when warm free or can grow under caps
sticky affinityKey re-acquires same boxId
two concurrent acquires never select same warm slot (synchronous stamp)
TWO concurrent growth decisions never exceed max (reservation counter, not boxId single-flight)
reservation released on provision reject (no permanent block)
maxInFlight>1 allows N leases on one box; N+1 blocks
maxBoxesPerIssue caps one issue's boxes so an ensemble cannot monopolize the pool
reconcile false->true grows from zero; true->false drains to zero
reconcile raising min/warm tops up; lowering max defers shrink to reaper (oldest-idle first), never destroys leased synchronously; never reconstructs the pool object
hydrate replays ledger + provider.list() and re-adopts boxes
hydrate force-returns orphan leased rows whose run is gone

drain is idempotent and resolves

snapshot reports total/warmIdle/leased/inFlight/spend/markedForDestroy accurately
ttl-exceeded WARM_IDLE above min destroyed
idle-exceeded above min destroyed; min floor respected
LEASED past ttl flagged markedForDestroy, NOT yanked, recycled when inFlight->0 inside mutex
probe failure on long-idle demotes to DEGRADED then DESTROYING
ORPHAN: stale-heartbeat lease whose run is gone is force-returned, clearing lease table AND decrementing inFlight
ALIVE: long single-turn run past staleHeartbeatMs but still active is NOT destroyed
list() authoritative: labeled pool-owned unknown -> destroyed
list() authoritative: unlabeled instance NEVER destroyed
registered-but-missing-in-list marked DESTROYED
top-up provisions toward min/warm only within spend budget
reaper serial: second tick during in-progress tick skipped
reaper timer handle.unref?.() invoked
provisional row flushed BEFORE provision
row UPSERTED with providerRef/workerHost after provision returns
load replays rows on hydrate
atomic write via tmp+rename

ledger untouched when usesLedger false
daily-spend sidecar persists boxSecondsToday across restart
daily-spend resets on UTC day-key change
passes shared conformance suite
provision idempotent on boxId
destroy idempotent (already gone)
injected probe failure -> ok:false
injected provision failure rejects
ZERO fs I/O (no ledger writes)
passes conformance suite over eval-ssh
reads ssh_hosts (snake_case) from providerOptions
reads sshHosts (camelCase) from providerOptions
probe runs runSsh printf-ready with worker.sshTimeoutMs
provision hands out hosts idempotently (min==max==len)
destroy forgets host, runs NO hooks, never deletes machine
inventory never exceeds max
inventory respects min floor when not draining
inFlight never negative
box-seconds monotonic per box
ledger-disabled providers never write disk
leaseId stale-release always a no-op
LEASED box never destroyed by reaper
two concurrent growth ops never exceed max (reservation invariant)
worker.box_pool parses all keys with snake_case aliasing
defaults applied when keys omitted
absent box_pool leaves settings.worker.boxPool undefined
REGRESSION: absent box_pool Settings clone deep-equals pre-change clone (byte-identical)
provider enum rejects unknown value
max < min throws
warm > max throws
negative/zero where positiveInt required throws
enabled+non-empty ssh_hosts throws anti-double-capacity error
static-ssh enabled without provider_options.ssh_hosts throws ssh_hosts-required
unknown key under box_pool throws (strict)
cloneSettings deep-copies providerOptions nested ssh_hosts array (mutating clone array element leaves original intact)
spend.* parsed in seconds; ttl/idle/drain in ms (unit split preserved)
providerOptions passed through un-normalized (ssh_hosts stays ssh_hosts)
workerCapacityAvailable consults capacityProbe.canAcquire when present
claim bypass sets workerHost to pending:// sentinel (never null) when probe present
claim bypass sets affinityHost = retry.workerHost (retry affinity preserved)
static sshHosts path unchanged when no probe
setWorkerHost overwrites the sentinel
abandonClaim deletes running+claimed with NO retry record and leaves slot re-claimable (distinct from finish backoff)
acquire called with affinityKey == prior real workerHost on retry (from affinityHost, not sentinel)
no_capacity -> runner NOT called, abandonClaim invoked (not finish), handle released, no history, no backoff scheduled
leased.workerHost written back via setWorkerHost before runner and passed to runner
history.workerHost == lease.workerHost
success path calls lease.release('healthy')
ssh_timeout error -> lease.fail (poison)
workspace_prepare_failed error -> lease.fail (poison)
ssh_not_found (local ENOENT) -> lease.release('healthy') (healthy box NOT recycled)
invalid_ssh_timeout / agent_run_aborted / ordinary agent failure -> lease.release('healthy')
stall-finished run (finishExternally) -> lease FAILED (poison), inFlight/spend correct
stale-generation late resolve -> lease op no-op (leaseId guard)
onUpdate triggers lease.heartbeat
reload calls boxPool.reconcile
reload throwing anti-double-capacity guard keeps last-good AND emits guard message in workflow_reload_failed
drainBoxPool awaits boxPool.drain
boxPool undefined => byte-identical regression (acquire/classifier never invoked)
pending:// sentinel visible in snapshot between claim and acquire; real host after acquire
memory-tracker daemon with fake pool leases a box and completes a run end to end
RunningEntry/history workerHost == fake://box-<id>
lease released and box returns to warm
pool at max surfaces worker_host_capacity (via canAcquire) with no claim-then-backoff churn
SIGINT path: drainBoxPool awaited, snapshot shows zero boxes
real acquire over loopback sshd returns leased box with real workerHost
runner (not pool) creates a real workspace over SSH
run executes and lease.release returns the box
lease.fail force-recycles a poisoned box
probe via runSsh printf-ready succeeds against live sshd
retry re-leases the SAME boxId and resume state is preserved (affinity not vacuous)
drain force-destroys remaining boxes (no leak)
multi-box allocation across >1 real host
per-box workspace isolation on distinct hosts
sticky re-acquire returns same host across a retry
resume continuity preserved when same boxId re-acquired

testMatrix.gating: per-provider live env gate (never default CI)
cases: cloud provider passes the SHARED conformance suite behind its gate
provision boots an instance and waits for SSH reachability via probe
list() enumerates only daemon-labeled instances
destroy terminates the instance
hydrate re-adopts survivors after a simulated restart, correlating provisional ledger rows by label
over-spend returns no_capacity:spend_cap before booting

edgeCases: Affinity NOT lost on bypass (verified gap fixed): claim() with capacityProbe sets RunningEntry.workerHost to a pending://<id>/<slot> sentinel AND RunningEntry.affinityHost = retry?.workerHost (retry.workerHost confirmed at orchestrator:218). runClaim reads affinity from affinityHost, never from the sentinel, so sticky re-acquire / resume continuity actually work.

Drain awaitable (verified gap fixed): stop() stays synchronous void (runtime:301-307) and only aborts handles. A new async drainBoxPool() awaits boxPool.drain and is invoked in main.ts's finally AFTER runtime.start() resolves (start() returns once stopped), since main.ts SIGINT handler (main.ts:125-127) cannot await stop(). This destroys paid cloud boxes before process exit.

No double-fired afterCreate (verified gap fixed): the pool NEVER runs workspace hooks. createWorkspaceForIssue already runs hooks.afterCreate (workspace:46-48,296-298) and the runner creates the per-run workspace (agent-runner:98-106). The pool's warm/teardown is box-lifecycle only (probe/destroy). The runner remains the sole workspace/hook owner, so afterCreate fires exactly once per run as today.

providerOptions snake_case reaches provider (verified gap fixed): the config normalizer is a flat per-key alias map that does NOT recurse into providerOptions. So providerOptions is intentionally passed through un-normalized and StaticSshBoxProvider reads BOTH ssh_hosts and sshHosts; a config test pins the passthrough and a provider test pins both spellings.

no_capacity recovery (verified gap fixed): a claim-time acquire failure calls orchestrator.abandonClaim (delete running+claimed, NO retry record) so the next poll re-evaluates capacity via canAcquire(). It does NOT call finish() (which schedules a backoff retry at orchestrator:207-221 and would churn) and does NOT produce a recordHistory. The worker_host_capacity surface is produced normally at the next poll-top via canAcquire (dispatch:66).

canAcquire is conservative, not a reservation (TOCTOU acknowledged + mitigated): eligibleIssues (orchestrator:74) and claim (orchestrator:104) both consult canAcquire, and N slots can race for the last box between eligibility and acquire. The loser hits no_capacity -> abandonClaim -> re-evaluated next tick (cheap, no backoff). This bounds churn to one wasted claim cycle per loser, not a retry storm.

SSH error classification by typed signal, not substring (verified gap fixed): classifyBoxOutcome returns 'poison' ONLY for box-transport faults (ssh_timeout, remote_home_lookup_failed, workspace_prepare_failed, ssh workspace-hook failure) and 'healthy' for ssh_not_found (local ENOENT - ssh binary missing, ssh:68,77), invalid_ssh_timeout (config), agent_run_aborted, and ordinary agent failures. A stall run, finished externally (finishExternally aborts -> runner throws agent_run_aborted), is classified 'poison' via a handle.reason='stalled' flag, NOT released-as-healthy.

Reaper orphan vs running runClaim (verified gap fixed): orphan-destroy and the later runClaim release coordinate through (a) the leaseId+settled+DESTROYED guard so the late release is a no-op AND (b) the per-box async mutex so inFlight is decremented exactly once. The reaper's orphan force-return clears the lease table entry and decrements inFlight itself, and the late release short-circuits.

Lease-held window during a hung session.stop() (verified gap acknowledged + bounded): runClaim's finally runs after the runner returns; the runner's own finally calls session.stop()+afterRun over SSH (agent-runner:179-194) which can block to sshTimeoutMs on a dead box. The lease is held until then. Backstops: heartbeat watchdog + ttlMs + the reaper orphan path (which clears the lease independently), and the eventual finally fail() is a leaseId-guarded no-op if the reaper already recycled it.

Two-phase workerHost sentinel observable (verified gap fixed): emit() fires after claim but before acquire (maybeDispatch:400). Using a pending:// sentinel instead of null means the TUI/HTTP snapshot never shows a misleading 'local'. A runtime test asserts the sentinel in the window and the real host after acquire.

Abort does not SIGKILL remote executors (verified gap acknowledged): handle.abort() rejects the in-progress turn (agent-runner:226) and throws between turns; it does not kill a remote SSH-spawned process. drain aborts handles then force-destroys; force-destroy on a cloud provider terminates the instance (killing the remote process), and resume state for an aborted run is invalidated by the existing reconcile/invalidate paths. For static-ssh, destroy is a no-op forget so no remote corruption.

Ledger crash-before-correlate (verified gap fixed): a provisional row is written BEFORE provision with the boxId; after provision returns the row is UPSERTED with providerRef/workerHost. A crash between create and response leaves a provisional row; hydrate reconciles via provider labels (provider must label at create) OR by matching the provisional boxId in metadata, and ttlMs is the universal backstop. Unlabeled instances are never destroyed.

Cold-start no_capacity (verified gap addressed): hydrate() runs once after construction; the first poll can fire before a real cloud box warms (provisioning >> acquireTimeoutMs). warm>=1 is honored at hydrate by kicking the reaper top-up immediately; until a box warms, canAcquire returns true only if growth is possible under caps, otherwise issues report worker_host_capacity and are re-evaluated each tick (no backoff). Documented as expected first-tick behavior for cold cloud pools.

dailyBoxSeconds reset + persistence (verified gap fixed): the daily cap uses a UTC day-key; box-seconds-today and the day-key persist to a spend.json sidecar next to the ledger so a daemon restart does not silently reset the daily total. Reset happens when the UTC day-key changes. A ledger test pins persistence + reset.

enabled flip during reload silently keeping last-good (verified gap fixed): a reload that sets box_pool.enabled with non-empty ssh_hosts throws the guard in parseConfig; reloadWorkflowIfConfigured (runtime:529-531) keeps last-good but now surfaces the guard's message in the workflow_reload_failed event so operators learn the two-capacity-source guard fired.

Ensemble monopolization (verified gap fixed): optional maxBoxesPerIssue caps how many boxes one issue's slots may hold concurrently, so an ensemble:N issue with maxInFlight=1 cannot consume the entire pool and starve others. The global FIFO waiter plus this per-issue cap provide basic fairness.

maxInFlight>1 + reverse-MCP tunnel (verified, default-safe): the MCP tunnel is keyed and refCounted purely on workerHost (mcp/src/agentEndpoint.ts:106-128, worker-host-pool:73-88), independent of the box pool. Recycling a box while a surviving run still references its tunnel is uncoordinated. Therefore maxInFlight defaults to 1 (only one run per box ever references the tunnel). maxInFlight>1 is gated behind a future verification test and documented as unsafe-by-default.

CloneSettings nested aliasing (verified gap fixed): shallow {...providerOptions} aliased the inner ssh_hosts array across the hot per-issue settingsForIssueState clone. cloneSettings now deep-copies providerOptions (structuredClone-equivalent) and spend; a config test mutates a clone's nested array and asserts the original is intact.

Reservation-based growth (verified gap fixed): boxId-keyed single-flight cannot dedupe two independently-chosen boxIds. Growth now takes a synchronous capacity reservation (reservedProvisions counter) BEFORE any await; count+reserved+inFlightBoxes is checked atomically so two concurrent growth decisions cannot both exceed max; the reservation is released on settle or provision-reject (so a rejected provision does not permanently block).

Orphaned provision after acquire timeout (verified gap addressed): a growth provision completing after its acquirer aborted becomes WARM_IDLE (reusable) under the reservation accounting; if abort cancels the provision midway and a half-created cloud instance exists, list()-reconcile adopts it by label (providers label at create) and ttlMs reaps it; the reservation is released so capacity is not leaked.
Unit split: warm/probe/home-lookup/validation uses worker.sshTimeoutMs; spend caps in seconds while ttl/idle/drain in ms - property-tested for unit correctness.
Misconfiguration two-capacity-sources: parse-time validation rejects enabled + non-empty ssh_hosts (throws). static-ssh enabled without provider_options.ssh_hosts is also rejected at parse time.
Unregistered/credential-missing provider: resolveProvider throws box_pool_provider_unavailable at daemon startup (fail loud before polling).
demoUsePhase: The 'use'/demo phase is the always-on end-to-end test ts/test/box-pool-e2e-memory.test.ts (T17) plus a documented manual run, both exercising the REAL runDaemon path with zero real SSH/cloud. Setup: tracker.kind=memory (memoryIssuesFromEnv via SYMPHONY_MEMORY_ISSUES), worker.box_pool.enabled=true provider=fake max=1 warm=1, a PATH-shimmed fake executor. Flow proven: (1) the daemon polls, the memory issue becomes eligible, orchestrator.workerCapacityAvailable consults boxPool.canAcquire (true), claim succeeds setting the pending:// sentinel; (2) runClaim reads affinity from affinityHost, acquires a lease, setWorkerHost overwrites the sentinel with fake://box-<id>, which is threaded into the runner (the RUNNER creates the workspace - the pool does not); (3) the run completes, the finally calls lease.release('healthy'), the box returns to WARM_IDLE; (4) snapshot/run history shows workerHost == fake://box-<id>, proving the pool produced the workerHost string end to end through the unchanged downstream pipeline; (5) the single box is filled at maxInFlight (or max=0-effective) so canAcquire returns false at eligibility and the issue is reported blocked with reason worker_host_capacity - and abandonClaim (not finish) ensures no backoff churn; (6) a SIGINT-equivalent stop() then drainBoxPool() is awaited and the snapshot shows zero boxes (the leak fix). Layered confidence above this demo: loopback-ssh (T18, SYMPHONY_TS_RUN_LIVE_SSH_E2E=1, setupNativeSshdWorker) proves real acquire -> runner-created workspace-over-SSH -> run -> release/fail against a real sshd AND end-to-end retry affinity (same boxId, preserved resume) AND drain-no-leak; BYO/docker proves multi-box; the shared provider-conformance suite (fake + static-ssh in CI, each cloud provider behind its own gate) proves a cloud provider plugs in identically. Final gate for every layer except live: `cd ts && mise run tidy` then `mise run check` (typecheck + test + lint) must pass with no commit/PR.
openQuestions: CRABBOX input was null: no crabbox artifact exists in the repo (only the worktree dir name ryan--crabbox). This plan is grounded entirely in source I read and verified, not a crabbox spec. If a real spec exists, re-feed it before locking the lease/provider contract.

Provider enum: PROVIDER_KINDS is ['fake','static-ssh','cloud'] with 'cloud' a single self-registering family selected by providerOptions.driver. Confirm whether each cloud (aws-ec2/fly) should instead be its own PROVIDER_KINDS member.

maxBoxesPerIssue default: left undefined (uncapped) for backward-compatible behavior, with the cap available for operators worried about ensemble monopolization. Confirm whether a sane default (e.g. max-1) should ship instead of uncapped.
Ledger + spend.json location: <workspace.root>/.symphony/box-pool/. Confirm writable in all deploy targets and no collision with the reverse-MCP-tunnel pool's state.

Cloud provider labeling guarantee: hydrate's crash-before-correlate recovery assumes the provider labels the instance atomically at create so unlabeled-orphan recovery works. This must be enforced by the cloud provider's conformance test; confirm the labeling contract before the first cloud driver.

Stall-as-poison classification: a stall-finished run marks the box poison (so it is recycled, since a stalled agent often left the box in a bad state). Confirm this is the desired policy vs recycling-as-healthy (some stalls are agent-side, not box-side).

drainDeadlineMs default 30s vs near-complete runs: a graceful drain aborts runs still in-flight past the deadline. Confirm 30s is acceptable, or whether it should be derived from agent.maxTurns * stall window for long runs.
Surfacing BoxPoolSnapshot through the observability HTTP/TUI projection: kept snapshot() internal/test-facing for now; confirm whether to expose it in this change or a follow-up.
